reasoning: Nana Dzagnidze is an established chess Grandmaster with a current rating of 2500 (August 2025 FIDE rating). At this level, Elo ratings are extremely stable. A 1% increase would require gaining at least 25 rating points (to 2525+), which is a massive jump for a 2500-level player and typically requires dominating a major tournament. Her live rating as of late August is reported as 2502, meaning she will likely be rated around 2502 on the September list. Because the FIDE list updates only on the 1st of each month, her rating on Sept 7 and Sept 30 will almost certainly be 2502, which is nowhere near the 2525 target (thus 5% probability, the system minimum). Over the medium term (November 2025) and longer term (February 2026), there is a slightly higher chance she plays an event or two where she heavily outperforms and gains 25 points, but as a long-time veteran, large upward swings are rare. I conservatively estimate 7% and 11% for those dates respectively.
